html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 咸通初 - Temporal marker indicating the early years of the Xiantong era (847–860 AD) during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 有道术 - Indicates possession of supernatural abilities or skills.
  • 养气绝粒 - Daoist practice of cultivating qi and abstaining from food.
  • 嗜酒耽棋 - Describes a love for drinking and playing chess.
  • 炉火药术 - Refers to alchemical practices, particularly the creation of elixirs.
  • 天大哂 - Poetic expression indicating the sky is laughing or mocking something.
  • 金乌 - Mythological term for the sun, often depicted as a three-legged crow.
  • 红炉谩烧药 - Metaphorical phrase suggesting the futility of burning alchemical substances.
  • 玉颜安可驻 - Means 'How can the beauty of a jade-like face be preserved?' referring to the inability to stop aging.
  • 今年花发枝，明年叶落树 - Metaphor for the transience of life, comparing the blooming and withering of flowers and leaves.
  • 不如且饮酒 - Suggests it is better to drink wine rather than worry about the passage of time.
  • 莫管流年逝 - Means 'Do not concern yourself with the passing of time.'
  • 门风常有蕙兰馨 - Metaphor for a family with a good reputation and noble character, using the fragrance of orchids and lilies.
  • 鼎族家传霸国名 - Indicates a family with a long-standing and prestigious lineage, tracing back to a powerful state.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 咸通初 - The early years of the Xiantong era, a period within the Tang Dynasty.
  • 进士 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the imperial examinations and is eligible for government service.
  • 金乌 - Mythological sun bird, often used in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 炉火药术 - Alchemy, a practice that was both scientific and mystical in ancient China, often associated with the pursuit of immortality.
  • 课得陈情二首 - Two poems composed by Zhang Chuo while he was detained.
  • 狄令 - The magistrate or local official who released Zhang Chuo.
  • 门风 - Family tradition or reputation.
  • 蕙兰馨 - Metaphor for a family with a good reputation, using the fragrance of orchids and lilies.
  • 鼎族 - A powerful and influential family.
  • 霸国名 - The name of a powerful state, likely referring to a historical dynasty or kingdom.

Proper Nouns

  • 张绰 - Zhang Chuo, the main subject of the text.
  • 咸通 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 狄令 - The name of the local official who released Zhang Chuo.
  • 蕙兰 - Orchids and lilies, used metaphorically to describe a family with a good reputation.
  • 鼎族 - A powerful and influential family.
  • 霸国名 - The name of a powerful state, likely referring to a historical dynasty or kingdom.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 容貌静悬秋月彩 - Poetic phrase describing a serene and beautiful appearance, likened to the color of the autumn moon.
  • 文章高振海涛声 - Metaphorical expression praising the brilliance and impact of one's writings, comparing them to the powerful sound of ocean waves.
  • 讼堂无事调琴轸 - Phrase describing a peaceful and harmonious situation in court, where disputes are resolved and music is played.
  • 郡阁何妨醉玉觥 - Phrase suggesting that even in official settings, one can enjoy life and indulge in pleasure, symbolized by drinking from a jade cup.
  • 今曰东渐桥下水，一条従此镇长清 - Metaphorical statement indicating that the influence of the person being described will spread and bring lasting peace and clarity to the region.
  • 何用梯媒向外求，长生只在内中修 - Philosophical statement suggesting that true longevity and spiritual growth come from inner cultivation rather than external means.
  • 自是行心不到头 - Phrase indicating that the person being described has not yet fully realized their potential or completed their journey.
  • 张绰张绰自不会，天下经书在腹内 - Self-deprecating statement indicating that despite having vast knowledge, the person being described feels inadequate.
  • 身却腾腾处世间，心即逍遥出天外 - Poetic expression describing a state of mind where one's body is active in the world, but their spirit is free and detached.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狄宰 - Refers to a local official named Di, who is the subject of the text.
  • 张之才 - Refers to Zhang, a person known for his talent and wisdom.
  • 明府勋贵家流 - Refers to a high-ranking official from a noble family.
  • 江南上昇 - Refers to a place or state of spiritual elevation in the Jiangnan region.
  • 东渐桥 - A bridge in the region, symbolizing a boundary or transition point.

Proper Nouns

  • 狄宰 - A local official named Di.
  • 张之才 - A person known for his talent and wisdom.
  • 明府 - A high-ranking official.
  • 江南 - The Jiangnan region, known for its cultural and spiritual significance.
  • 东渐桥 - A bridge in the region.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太尉朱崖出镇浙右 - A set phrase introducing the main character and his role as a high-ranking military and political official governing the right side of the Zhejiang region.
  • 常住什物 - Refers to the property or assets of a temple or monastery.
  • 被前主事隐用 - Means 'was secretly used by the previous officer.'
  • 却常住金若干两 - '却' here means 'but' or 'however,' and '常住金' refers to the temple's gold. '若干两' means 'a certain number of taels.'
  • 引证前数辈 - Means 'cited several predecessors.'
  • 皆有递相交割文字分明 - '递相交割' means 'passed on in succession,' and '文字分明' means 'clearly documented in writing.'
  • 鞫成具狱，伏罪昭昭 - '鞫成' means 'investigated and completed,' '具狱' means 'case file,' and '伏罪昭昭' means 'clearly guilty.'
  • 未穷破用之所由 - '未穷' means 'not fully investigated,' '破用' means 'spent,' and '所由' means 'reason or cause.'
  • 僧人不拘细行而费之 - '不拘细行' means 'not bound by minor details,' and '费之' means 'spent on it.'
  • 无理可伸，甘之死地 - '无理可伸' means 'no reason to argue,' and '甘之死地' means 'willing to die rather than admit guilt.'
  • 引虑之际，公疑其未尽 - '引虑' means 'during the deliberation,' and '公疑其未尽' means 'the public official suspected it was not fully investigated.'
  • 徵以意揣之髡人乃具实以闻 - '徵以意' means 'probed with intent,' '揣之' means 'probed him,' '髡人' means 'a monk,' and '具实以闻' means 'reported the truth.'
  • 居寺者乐于知事 - '居寺者' means 'those living in the temple,' '乐于' means 'enjoy,' and '知事' means 'officer.'
  • 前后主之者，积年已来，空交分两文书，其实无金 - '前后主之者' means 'those who have been in charge before,' '积年已来' means 'for many years,' '空交分两文书' means 'empty documents of weight and value,' and '其实无金' means 'in reality, there was no gold.'
  • 众以某孤立，不杂辈流，欲乘此挤排之 - '众以某孤立' means 'the crowd believes he is isolated,' '不杂辈流' means 'does not mix with others,' and '欲乘此挤排之' means 'wants to take this opportunity to exclude him.'
  • 因流涕不胜其冤 - '因流涕' means 'shed tears,' and '不胜其冤' means 'cannot bear the injustice.'
  • 立従召兜子数乘 - '立従' means 'immediately ordered,' '召' means 'summon,' '兜子' means 'palanquin,' and '数乘' means 'several.'
  • 命关连僧入对事 - '命' means 'ordered,' '关连僧' means 'involved monks,' and '入对事' means 'to enter and face the case.'
  • 咸遣蔽帘子，毕令门不相对 - '咸遣' means 'all were sent,' '蔽帘子' means 'with screens,' and '毕令门不相对' means 'so that they could not see each other.'
  • 命取黄泥，各令模前后交付下次金样，以凭证据 - '命取' means 'ordered to fetch,' '黄泥' means 'yellow clay,' '各令模' means 'each was ordered to mold,' '前后交付' means 'before and after delivery,' '下次金样' means 'the next sample of gold,' and '以凭证据' means 'to serve as evidence.'
  • 竟模不成 - '竟模' means 'finally molded,' and '不成' means 'did not succeed.'
  • 公怒令劾前辈，皆一一伏罪 - '公怒' means 'the official was angry,' '令劾' means 'ordered to accuse,' '前辈' means 'predecessors,' and '皆一一伏罪' means 'all admitted their guilt one by one.'
  • 其所排者遂获清雪 - '其所排者' means 'those who were excluded,' and '遂获清雪' means 'finally cleared of the charge.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太尉 - A high-ranking military and political title in ancient China.
  • 朱崖 - A personal name, likely a historical figure.
  • 浙右 - Refers to the right side of the Zhejiang region, an area in southeastern China.
  • 常住什物 - Property or assets of a temple or monastery.
  • 僧人 - Monks or religious figures in Buddhism.
  • 知事 - An officer or administrator in a temple.
  • 黄泥 - Yellow clay, often used for molding or casting.
  • 清雪 - To clear a charge or be exonerated.

Proper Nouns

  • 太尉 - A high-ranking military and political title.
  • 朱崖 - A personal name.
  • 浙右 - A geographical region.
  • 常住什物 - Property or assets of a temple.
  • 僧人 - Monks.
  • 知事 - Officers in a temple.
  • 黄泥 - Yellow clay.
  • 清雪 - Exoneration.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自称侠 - Self-proclaimed '侠' (xiá), referring to a chivalrous or heroic figure in Chinese culture, often associated with righteousness and bravery.
  • 下第后 - Refers to failing the imperial examinations, a significant event in the lives of scholars.
  • 江淮 - Refers to the regions of Jiangsu and Anhui provinces, historically known for their cultural significance.
  • 嗜酒 - Means 'loves drinking' or 'is fond of alcohol.'
  • 侮谑时辈 - Means 'mocks and teases contemporaries,' indicating a playful but sometimes disrespectful attitude.
  • 乘饮兴 - Means 'when inspired by drinking,' suggesting a state of heightened emotion or creativity due to alcohol.
  • 出门便与妻儿别 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'leaves home immediately,' emphasizing the readiness to act on impulse.
  • 播在人口 - Means 'spread by word of mouth,' indicating that something is widely discussed or known.
  • 牢盆使 - Refers to the official responsible for managing salt production and distribution, a significant economic role.
  • 冬瓜 - A colloquial term for a small official position, possibly a pun or metaphor.
  • 椿儿绕树春园里，桂子寻花夜月中 - A poetic description of two sons, with '椿儿' and '桂子' being names or nicknames, possibly symbolizing growth and beauty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崔涯、张祜 - Historical figures, both poets and scholars who lived during the Tang Dynasty. Their reputations and works are well-documented.
  • 进士 - A degree awarded to successful candidates in the imperial examinations, indicating high academic achievement.
  • 江淮 - Historically significant regions in China, known for their cultural and economic importance.
  • 侠 - A cultural archetype in Chinese literature and history, representing a person who acts with courage and righteousness, often outside the bounds of official law.
  • 牢盆使 - An important official position related to salt production, which was a major source of state revenue.
  • 冬瓜 - A colloquial term for a small official position, possibly a pun or metaphor.

Proper Nouns

  • 崔涯 - A historical figure, a poet and scholar.
  • 张祜 - Another historical figure, a poet and scholar.
  • 江淮 - Refers to the regions of Jiangsu and Anhui provinces.
  • 牢盆使 - An official position related to salt production.
  • 冬瓜 - A colloquial term for a small official position.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 薄有资力 - Means 'to have a little wealth or resources.'
  • 非常人 - Refers to someone who is not ordinary or is unusual.
  • 装饰甚武 - Means 'dressed in a very martial manner.'
  • 腰剑 - Means 'wearing a sword at the waist.'
  • 手囊贮一物 - Means 'carrying a bag containing an object.'
  • 流血于外 - Means 'with blood flowing outside the bag.'
  • 入门谓曰 - Means 'upon entering, he said to [Zhang].'
  • 然 - Means 'yes' or 'indeed.'
  • 揖客甚谨 - Means 'greeted the guest very respectfully.'
  • 既坐 - Means 'after sitting down.'
  • 有酒否 - Means 'do you have any wine?'
  • 乃扶囊烛下 - Means 'then took the bag under the candlelight.'
  • 筹其缣素中品之物 - Means 'counted out some medium-quality silk and cloth.'
  • 量而与之 - Means 'measured and gave it to him.'
  • 快哉 - Means 'how delightful!'
  • 无所恨也 - Means 'there is nothing to regret.'
  • 期以却回 - Means 'promised to return later.'
  • 五鼓绝声 - Means 'at the fifth drumbeat, the sound ceased.'
  • 东曦既驾 - Means 'the sun has already risen in the east.'
  • 杳无踪迹 - Means 'vanished without a trace.'
  • 虑以囊首彰露 - Means 'worried that the bag containing the head might be exposed.'
  • 且非己为 - Means 'and it was not done by me.'
  • 计将安出 - Means 'what should I do now?'
  • 遣家人将欲埋之 - Means 'sent family members to bury it.'
  • 开囊出之 - Means 'opened the bag and took it out.'
  • 乃豕首矣 - Means 'it turned out to be a pig's head.'
  • 因方悟之 - Means 'then suddenly realized.'
  • 虚其名，无其实 - Means 'having a false reputation but no substance.'
  • 而见欺之若是 - Means 'and was deceived in such a way.'
  • 不可戒欤 - Means 'how can one not be wary?'
  • 豪侠之气 - Means 'the spirit of a bold and chivalrous person.'
  • 自此而丧矣 - Means 'and lost it from then on.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张侠士 - Refers to Zhang, a chivalrous or heroic figure.
  • 仇人 - Refers to an enemy or someone one has a grudge against.
  • 义士 - Refers to a righteous or noble person.
  • 十万缗 - Refers to a large sum of money, specifically ten thousand strings of cash.
  • 赴汤蹈火 - A metaphor for going through fire and water, meaning to face danger without hesitation.
  • 为狗为鸡 - A metaphor for doing anything, no matter how lowly or menial.
  • 虚其名，无其实 - Refers to having a false reputation but no substance.
  • 豪侠之气 - Refers to the spirit of a bold and chivalrous person.

Proper Nouns

  • 张侠士 - Refers to Zhang, a chivalrous or heroic figure.
  • 仇人 - Refers to an enemy or someone one has a grudge against.
  • 义士 - Refers to a righteous or noble person.
  • 十万缗 - Refers to a large sum of money, specifically ten thousand strings of cash.
  • 赴汤蹈火 - A metaphor for going through fire and water, meaning to face danger without hesitation.
  • 为狗为鸡 - A metaphor for doing anything, no matter how lowly or menial.
  • 虚其名，无其实 - Refers to having a false reputation but no substance.
  • 豪侠之气 - Refers to the spirit of a bold and chivalrous person.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 堂堂 - Means 'majestic' or 'grand'.
  • 重光 - Means 'dazzling light'.
  • 点去冰旁 - Means 'remove the ice side' (referring to the character '水').
  • 二人相连 - Means 'two people connected' (referring to the character '天').
  • 不欠一边 - Means 'not lacking one side' (referring to the character '下').
  • 三梁四柱烈火燃 - Means 'three beams and four pillars burning fiercely' (referring to the character '无').
  • 添却双勾两曰全 - Means 'add double hooks and two days complete' (referring to the character '比').

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太保令狐相 - A high-ranking official, likely a historical figure.
  • 大明寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Chang'an (modern Xi'an), known for its cultural and religious significance.
  • 支使班蒙 - An assistant or subordinate, likely a historical figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄绢之奇智 - A reference to the intelligence of the person who solved the riddle, possibly alluding to a famous historical figure known for his wisdom.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 按辔躬已而治之 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to personally take charge and govern' (literally: 'gripping the reins and personally governing').
  • 补缀颓毁，整葺坏纲 - Compound verbs indicating the restoration of damaged structures and the reorganization of disordered systems.
  • 功无虚曰 - Rare or archaic expression. Possibly meaning 'no day passed without achievement' or 'every day saw accomplishments.'
  • 萃其所 - Likely means 'gathering it all together' or 'concentrating it in one place.'
  • 芳春九旬 - Poetic way of saying a long period of spring, likely meaning a period of about three months.
  • 都人士女 - Refers to the people of the capital city, both men and women. Should be translated as 'the citizens of the capital, both men and women.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 赏心亭 - Name of the pavilion mentioned in the passage. Should be retained as 'Shanxin Pavilion' or 'Pavilion of Delightful Hearts.'
  • 戏马亭 - Famous pavilion in Xuzhou, associated with the historical figure Liu Yu. Should be translated as 'Xima Pavilion.'
  • 玉钩斜道 - Poetic or archaic name for a road or path. Should be translated as 'Jade Hook Sloping Road' or 'Jade Hook Path.'
  • 度支 - Tang government term for the finance ministry or treasury, responsible for grain and monetary administration. Should be translated as 'the Ministry of Finance' or 'the Treasury.'
  • 薛阳陶 - Proper name. Should be transliterated as 'Xue Yangtao' or 'Xie Yangtao' depending on historical evidence.
  • 朱崖 - Likely refers to Zhu Ya, a historical figure or a place. Should be transliterated as 'Zhu Ya.'

Proper Nouns

  • 咸通 - Xiantong (era name of the Tang Dynasty).
  • 丞相姑臧公 - Chancellor Guzang (or Lord Guzang).
  • 端揆 - The highest official position, often the Chancellor or Prime Minister.
  • 彭门 - Pengmen, likely a place name (possibly Pengcheng, modern Xuzhou).
  • 薛阳陶 - Xue Yangtao.
  • 朱崖 - Zhu Ya.
  • 戏马亭 - Xima Pavilion.
  • 玉钩斜道 - Jade Hook Sloping Road.
  • 度支 - Ministry of Finance / Treasury.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一曰 - Temporal marker meaning 'one day' or 'on one occasion,' used to introduce a specific event or anecdote.
  • 公召陶同游 - Official invited Tao to travel together.
  • 问及往曰芦管之事 - Asked about a specific event or story involving a reed pipe (芦管).
  • 陶因献朱崖陆鬯元白所撰歌一曲 - Tao presented a song composed by individuals or groups named Zhu Ya, Lu Chang, Yuan Bai.
  • 公亦喜之，即于兹亭奏之 - Official also found it pleasing and immediately played it in this pavilion.
  • 其管绝徵 - Reed pipe of the highest quality.
  • 每于一觱篥管中，常容三管也 - Within the pipe, it can accommodate three pipes.
  • 声如天际，自然而来 - Sound is like it comes from the heavens, naturally arises.
  • 情思宽<门夕> - Emotions and thoughts are open or expansive.
  • 公大隹赏之 - Official highly praised it.
  • 亦赠其诗，不记终篇 - Also gave him a poem, but the end of the poem is not remembered.
  • 其发端云 - Beginning of the poem says.
  • 虚心纤质雁衔余，凤吹龙吟定不如 - Humble and delicate nature, metaphor, sound of phoenixes and dragons.
  • 於是赐赍甚丰 - Therefore, gifts and rewards were abundant.
  • 出其二子，皆授牢盆倅职 - Sent out his two sons, all given a specific official position.
  • 诸従事以公近讳，盖赏字有尚也 - Various officials because of the official's recent taboo, it seems that the character '赏' is still in use.
  • 遂不改作 - Therefore, did not change it.
  • 其亭自秦毕陷逆，乃为刍豢之地 - Pavilion became a place for fodder and livestock from the time of Qin's defeat.
  • 嗟乎！公孙弘之东阁，刘屈_后为马厩，亦何异哉！ - Sorrowfully, Gong Sun Hong's east pavilion, later became a stable, what is different about this?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 公 - Refers to a high-ranking official or noble, likely from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陶 - Likely refers to Tao Yuanming or another literatus.
  • 朱崖陆鬯元白 - Refers to individuals or groups who composed the song.
  • 芦管 - Type of reed instrument, often associated with music and poetry.
  • 觱篥 - Traditional Chinese wind instrument, similar to an oboe, used in court music.
  • 宣父 - Respectful term for Confucius.
  • 公孙弘之东阁 - Specific place associated with Gong Sun Hong, a chancellor during the Han Dynasty.
  • 刘屈_后为马厩 - Place that was once a stable, historical reference to a change in the use of a building.

Proper Nouns

  • 公 - Refers to a high-ranking official or noble, likely from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陶 - Likely refers to Tao Yuanming or another literatus.
  • 朱崖陆鬯元白 - Refers to individuals or groups who composed the song.
  • 宣父 - Respectful term for Confucius.
  • 公孙弘之东阁 - Specific place associated with Gong Sun Hong.
  • 刘屈_后为马厩 - Place that was once a stable.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兩出鎮於浙右 - Governed the eastern part of Zhejiang twice.
  • 奉詔西行 - Obeying the imperial decree to travel west.
  • 祗別和尚 - Farewell to the monk.
  • 多空教所長 - Skilled in Buddhist teachings.
  • 亟令取之，須臾而至 - Quickly ordered to fetch it, arrived shortly.
  • 規圓而漆之 - Rounded off and painted with lacquer.
  • 不復目其僧矣 - No longer looked at the monk with the same respect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太尉 - High-ranking official in the imperial court, often equivalent to a modern-day minister of defense.
  • 朱崖公 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official or nobleman from the region of Zhu Ya (modern-day Hainan or Fujian).
  • 浙右 - Refers to the eastern part of Zhejiang province.
  • 甘露寺 - Famous temple in Zhejiang, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 筇竹杖 - Type of walking stick made from bamboo, often associated with scholars and officials.
  • 老僧 - Refers to an elderly monk, often respected for their wisdom and experience.

Proper Nouns

  • 太尉 - High-ranking official title.
  • 朱崖公 - Historical figure.
  • 浙右 - Geographical region.
  • 甘露寺 - Famous temple.
  • 筇竹杖 - Type of walking stick.
  • 老僧 - Elderly monk.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 書空有換鵝之能 - Refers to the ability to exchange calligraphy for geese, referencing the famous anecdote about Wang Xizhi.
  • 書空跷足睡，路險側身行 - Describes a set of behaviors: writing in the air, sleeping with feet up, dangerous roads, and sideways walking.
  • 彌天四海之談 - Indicates wide-ranging and profound discussions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太尉 - Refers to the Grand Commandant, a high-ranking official in ancient China.
  • 大宛国 - Refers to the ancient region of Dayuan, located in present-day Fergana Valley in Uzbekistan.
  • 蘭若 - Refers to Buddhist temples or monasteries.
  • 先達 - Refers to respected predecessors or scholars.

Proper Nouns

  • 太尉 - The Grand Commandant, a high-ranking official.
  • 大宛國 - The ancient region of Dayuan.
  • 蘭若 - Buddhist temples or monasteries.
  • 先達 - Respected predecessors or scholars.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 好饮绝粒 - Compound phrase meaning 'loved drinking and abstained from food.'
  • 巡坐求饮 - Means 'roamed around seeking drinks,' describing a habitual behavior.
  • 盖自量其得所 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'he measured his own needs and desires.'
  • 列酒旗于城街之西 - Means 'set up wine banners on the western street of the city,' indicating a place of business.
  • 阴雨往诸旗亭不及 - Means 'when it rained heavily, he couldn't reach the flag pavilions,' referring to places where travelers could rest and drink.
  • 可均有所求，亦不造矣 - Means 'if Du Keming had something to ask for, he wouldn't go there,' indicating a condition.
  • 或无所获，必乃过之 - Means 'if he didn't get anything, he would definitely pass by,' describing a habitual action.
